items.title: Rapid KL deploys extra staff, keeps free buses and alternative trains running as Ampang-Sri Petaling repair works continue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Rapid KL said alternative train services and free shuttle buses will remain active until the end of today following the derailment near Chan Sow Lin station that disrupted services on the Ampang–Sri Petaling Line earlier this morning. “Repair works are still actively ongoing,” Rapid KL said, adding that “alternative train services and shuttle buses remain active until the end of operating hours”. It also activated several shuttle train services between affected stations, including Bandar Tasik Selatan and Cheras, Ampang and Maluri, as well as Maluri and Chan Sow Lin.

items.title: GOF raids in Kelantan uncover drugs, undocumented migrants and RM1.1m timber haul
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 28 — The General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade carried out four separate operations involving drugs, illegal immigrants, ketum juice and illegal logging activities in Kelantan, with total seizures amounting to RM1.93 million. Its commander, SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain, said the operations were conducted under “Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan” (OTWK) and “Op Bersepadu Khazanah” (OBK), involving cooperation with various enforcement agencies. Ahmad Radzi said in the second case, 19 Myanmar nationals, comprising 13 men and six women aged between 17 and 40, were detained near Kampung Jeram Perdah, Pasir Mas on May 20.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan DKU says no special meeting on May 29 as matter now before courts
items.summary: In the letter dated May 26, DKU secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin said the proposed special meeting requested by several Negeri Sembilan royal and customary leaders could not be convened due to ongoing court proceedings. “DKU is unable to convene a special meeting as requested in view of the ongoing court proceedings,” he wrote. Raja Norazli also said the individuals involved could not independently convene or call for a DKU meeting, adding that any purported special meeting scheduled for May 29 would not be recognised as an official DKU meeting.

items.title: Salesman charged with CBT for misappropriating RM500,000 jewellery for gambling
items.summary: Investigations found that the accused, who had been entrusted to sell newly produced gold to various shops, instead pawned the items to obtain money for gambling. Police also found that a total of 1.4kg of gold entrusted to the accused for sale resulted in losses exceeding RM700,000 based on current gold prices. Inspector Aizuddin Sulaiman conducted the prosecution, while lawyer Moganesya Nagendran represented the accused.

items.title: T7 Global's net profit rises 2.8pct in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: T7 Global Bhd 's net profit gained 2.8 per cent to RM7.24 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 from RM7.05 million a year ago lifted by the industrial solutions segment. The company logged a revenue of RM184.56 million in the quarter under review, up 32.9 per cent year-on-year from RM138.88 million in the same period last year. In a statement, the company said that its industrial solutions segment delivered a significant revenue growth of 142 per cent to RM89.2 million.

items.title: Malaysian man charged with murder of Singaporean woman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Malaysian man was charged in Singapore on Tuesday with the murder of a woman at a Housing and Development Board (HDB) block in the republic's Choa Chu Kang district. Mohamad Faiz Umar, 22, was accused of murdering Chua Bee Ting, 21, inside a lift at Block 248 Choa Chu Kang Avenue 2 on May 26. Upon arrival, police found Chua motionless at the lift lobby on the 12th floor.

items.title: Search for boy swept away in Sungai Bernam enters second day
items.summary: KUALA KUBU BARU: Search and rescue operations for a 10-year-old boy feared drowned after being swept away by strong currents at Sungai Bernam near Kampung Sungai Nilam here entered their second day today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said rescuers resumed operations this morning, focusing surface searches within a 3km radius from the point where the victim was believed to have fallen into the river. "Firefighters from the Kuala Kubu Baru fire station arrived at the scene at 2.35pm before immediately launching search efforts," he said today.

items.title: Delivery man pleads not guilty to threatening salon assistant in Sentul
items.summary: A. Vinodvarma, 36, is accused of committing the act against Ashha Begum, 24, at Taman Bamboo, Off Jalan Ipoh, Sentul, at 6.20pm on May 22. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Amritpreet Kaur Randhawa did not offer bail to the accused, as the offence falls under the non-bailable category. However, Vinodvarma’s lawyer, Erickka Farrise Amir, sought bail, saying that her client is the family’s breadwinner and poses no flight risk.

items.title: Kelantan Sultan donates two Brahman cattle for qurban programme
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Sultan of Kelantan Sultan Muhammad V has donated two Brahman cattle weighing nearly one tonne each for the Jama'ie Qurban Programme today. Held at the Sultan Ismail Petra Islamic Complex in Panji, the programme was organised by the Kelantan Islamic Religious and Malay Customs Council (MAIK). MAIK deputy president Datuk Mohd Anis Hussein said the two cattle were among 55 slaughtered today in conjunction with Aidiladha.

items.title: #NSTviral: Viral dashcam video shows motorcycle hitting stopped SUV in emergency lane [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral dashcam video showing a motorcycle crashing into an SUV along an emergency lane has raised concerns over highway safety and visibility on high-speed roads. Based on the footage, the SUV's brake lights and hazard lights did not appear to be activated, while no warning triangle or safety cones were seen placed behind the vehicle, prompting debate over whether adequate safety precautions had been taken. user @Ahmad Nazmi questioned.

items.title: Thirteen-year-old girl allegedly beaten by group of students after ‘misunderstanding’, say Kota Kinabalu police
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Kota Kinabalu police are investigating the alleged assault of a 13-year-old girl who was reportedly beaten by five female students in an incident that surfaced after a video went viral on social media. Police said they received a report from the victim at about 7.51am on May 23, a day after the incident allegedly took place in the city. Initial investigations found the girl was allegedly assaulted by five students from several different schools in the Kota Kinabalu area following what police described as a misunderstanding and lingering dissatisfaction among those involved.

items.title: Hamzah accused of plotting Bersatu takeover, allegedly with Pas, Umno backing
items.summary: Marzuki said Bersatu's constitution stipulates that the deputy president assumes the role of acting president if the president steps down. "Become deputy president uncontested, then pressure Muhyiddin to resign. After Muhyiddin resigns, he (Hamzah) becomes acting president of Bersatu.

items.title: Not everybody is a loser from the Iran war. Just ask Brunei
items.summary: But there are countries where higher prices for crude oil, refined products and liquefied natural gas (LNG) are providing huge benefits that, so far, outweigh the cost of rising inflation. Brunei's exports of crude oil were 2.74 million barrels in April, the most in three months and up 51 per cent from the 1.81 million barrels in April 2025, according to data compiled by commodity analysts Kpler. Kpler estimates Brunei's shipments of refined products will hit 4.16 million barrels in May, the most since July last year, with Australia, the world's biggest importer of diesel, the top destination.

items.title: Submissions for phase four of Bantuan Kasih Johor extended until June 28, says MB
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 28 — The Johor state government has extended the period for the submission of Bantuan Kasih Johor (BKJ) Fourth Phase until June 28 to give more time to recipients to obtain the assistance. “At least 35 review counters and 35 payment counters will be provided for locations with more than 3,000 recipients, while locations with 2,000 recipients and below will involve 10 review counters and 10 payment counters,” he said in a statement after chairing the Fourth Phase BKJ Coordination Meeting here. Onn Hafiz said the operating hours would also be extended to 12 hours, especially in the Johor Bahru district, to reduce congestion and provide comfort to recipients, especially the elderly.

items.title: Nine hurt, including suspected migrant smuggler, after GOF car chase ends in crash near Jeli
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Nine men were injured, three of them seriously, after a car believed to be transporting undocumented migrants crashed near Kampung Sungai Rual in Jeli, Kelantan, this morning. Southeast Brigade General Operations Force commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the remaining victims suffered minor injuries and were treated at Jeli Hospital. He said a General Operations Force (GOF) team from the Seventh Battalion had spotted a suspicious Proton Saga on the East-West Highway.

items.title: P-hailing rider claims trial to issuing death threat against shop assistant
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A p-hailing rider pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court today to criminally intimidating a hairdressing shop assistant with a death threat last week. A. Vinodvarma, 36, entered the plea after the charge was read before magistrate Farah Nabihah Muhamad Dan. However, lawyer Erickka Farrise Amir urged the court to exercise its discretion and grant minimum bail.

items.title: AI chiefs walk back job apocalypse warnings
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The most prominent figures in artificial intelligence (AI) are stepping back from dire predictions about mass unemployment, as the industry faces growing public hostility over AI's promised transformation of the workplace. Speaking to Channel News Asia on Monday, Huang took direct aim at fellow executives who have publicly blamed AI for workforce reductions. "AI has just arrived.

items.title: Man's appeal against 12-year sentence for assaulting stepdaughters dismissed
items.summary: Judge Datuk Azmi Ariffin, who has since been elevated to the Federal Court, wrote in a 22-page judgment that the 34-year-old appellant had betrayed the victims' trust and that his late regret carried no weight. Azmi, sitting alongside Datuk Meor Hashimi Abdul Hamid and Datuk Seri Mohd Radzi Harun, described sexual crimes as disgusting, despicable, and shameful. He said the victims, aged 16 and 17, were sexually assaulted whenever their mother, a school teacher, was away from home.

items.title: UOA Group co-founder CS Kong passes away at 85
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UOA Group co-founder and UOA Development Bhd executive director and managing director (MD) Kong Chong Soon, better known as CS Kong, has passed away at the age of 85. In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, UOA Development said Kong passed away peacefully on May 27, 2026. UOA Development chairman Kong Pak Lim said Kong had served the company with "exceptional distinction and integrity", providing steadfast leadership and strategic direction throughout his tenure as managing director.

items.title: ‘Does not tolerate disrespect’: FamilyMart Malaysia suspends employee over viral soup-throwing row with delivery rider
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — FamilyMart Malaysia has suspended an employee accused of throwing soup at a delivery rider following a dispute captured in a viral social media video, according to FMT. “Our team is investigating the incident with urgency and the staff (allegedly) involved has been placed off duty,” FamilyMart Malaysia said in a statement to the media organisation. The incident surfaced after a 23-second video began circulating online today showing a heated exchange between a delivery rider and a FamilyMart employee.

items.title: CIMB shares rise despite softer quarterly earnings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Group Holdings Bhd shares rose in early trade today despite posting lower earnings in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (1Q 2026). In a Bursa Malaysia filing, the bank said its net profit fell by 2.9 per cent to RM1.91 billion in 1Q 2026 from RM1.97 billion a year ago, while revenue eased by 1.6 per cent to RM5.41 billion from RM5.49 billion previously. CIMB said its net profit translated into a return on equity (ROE) of 11 per cent and earnings per share of 17.8 sen.

items.title: In Sarawak, three men arrested for suspected trespass into house compound in Tudan Phase 3
items.summary: MIRI, May 28 — Police arrested three local men aged between 21 and 31 on Monday for suspected trespassing into the compound of a house in Tudan Phase 3 here. Miri district police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said police received a report on Sunday night after the homeowner encountered the suspects at his residence at around 9.30pm. Acting on information received, a team from the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) of the Miri District Police Headquarters arrested the three men at Kampung Tudan here.

items.title: Rafizi dismisses talk of Nurul Izzah joining Bersama
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has denied claims that PKR deputy president Nurul Izzah Anwar will join Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama). The former Pandan member of parliament described the claim as a baseless rumour. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: [UPDATED] Rafizi denies RM8.5mil claim over Bersama takeover
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersama co-founder Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has denied allegations that RM8.5 million was paid to take over the political party, describing the claim as slander. Rafizi said claims surrounding the alleged payment were false and politically motivated. Rafizi also said the party had surpassed 20,000 members within two weeks, in line with expectations.

items.title: Maybank posts resilient RM2.48bil Q1 profit amid market volatility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) delivered a resilient first-quarter performance for the period ended March 31, 2026, posting a net profit of RM2.48 billion despite softer trading income amid volatile market conditions. While net profit eased 4.2 per cent from RM2.58 billion a year earlier, the country's largest bank by assets said earnings remained supported by stronger net interest margins, prudent cost management and stable asset quality. Non-interest income declined to RM1.99 billion, while net operating income fell to RM7.10 billion from RM7.71 billion previously.

items.title: Malaysian, 22, charged with murder after woman found dead in Singapore HDB lift
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — A 22-year-old Malaysian man has been charged with murder after a 21-year-old woman was found dead at a Housing and Development Board (HDB) block in Singapore’s Choa Chu Kang estate earlier this week. According to The Straits Times (ST), Mohamad Faiz Umar was charged in court today over the death of Chua Bee Ting, who was allegedly killed inside a lift at Block 248 Choa Chu Kang Avenue 2 on the night of May 26. Preliminary police investigations indicated that Faiz had allegedly stabbed Chua.

items.title: Drowned man's body found in Cheras river after public tip-off
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have confirmed the discovery of an unidentified male body believed to have drowned in Sungai Kerayong near Jalan Cheras. Cheras police said they received information from the public around 5.13pm yesterday regarding the discovery of the body. Police said he was believed to have drowned two to three days earlier.

items.title: DoE issues RM4.59m in fines after more than 5,000 inspections in first four months of 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 28 — The Department of Environment (DOE) conducted more than 5,000 inspections nationwide and recorded accumulated fines amounting to RM4.59 million involving environmental pollution cases during the first four months of this year. “Based on the inspections and investigations conducted, the DOE has taken 3,149 follow-up enforcement actions against parties found to be non-compliant with the Environmental Quality Act 1974,” the statement said. In efforts to curb the illegal entry of scheduled waste into the country, DOE said 51 inspection operations were conducted at major ports involving inspections on 702 containers suspected of carrying scheduled waste.

items.title: June rollout of fentanyl test kits signals tougher stance on synthetic drugs, says Nada D-G
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) will begin using specialised testing kits nationwide next month to detect fentanyl and other emerging synthetic drugs, amid mounting concerns over substances being mixed into vape liquids. Ruslin said the kits were capable of detecting fentanyl, synthetic cannabinoids, cocaine and psilocybin — the psychoactive compound found in so-called “magic mushrooms”. “Previously, there were claims that Nada could not test for fentanyl and synthetic substances.

items.title: Matrix Concepts posts record FY26 sales, higher profit, declares 1.25 sen dividend
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd recorded all-time high sales of RM1.51 billion for the financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26), driven by strong industrial land sales at its Malaysian Vision Valley City (MVV City) development and strong demand across its core townships. The sales were 9.2 per cent higher compared to last year's RM1.38 billion, largely supported by industrial land transactions at MVV City, which contributed RM362.6 million in sales during the year. Matrix Concepts' flagship Sendayan Developments township in Negeri Sembilan remained its largest contributor, generating RM884.6 million in sales as demand continued for its integrated and value-driven developments.

items.title: Man reportedly gored to death by runaway buffalo in Johor, attack video goes viral
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — A man was reportedly gored to death by a runaway buffalo in an incident believed to have taken place in the Ulu Tiram area yesterday, with footage of the attack later circulating widely on social media. The victim was later seen getting back to his feet following the attack, although he is understood to have suffered serious injuries. Online , he later died from injuries sustained in the incident.

items.title: AmBank raises dividend payout on higher earnings for FY26
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AMMB Holdings Bhd's (AmBank) net profit rose five per cent to RM2.1 billion in the financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26) from RM2 billion a year ago. Revenue for FY26 rose to RM5.16 billion from RM4.92 billion a year ago. Revenue rose to RM1.3 billion from RM1.28 billion.

items.title: Trump refiles US$10 billion defamation suit against WSJ over report on Epstein ties
items.summary: NEW YORK: President Donald Trump on Wednesday refiled a defamation lawsuit seeking at least US$10 billion in damages against the Wall Street Journal over its reporting on his ties to Jeffrey Epstein, after a judge threw out an earlier version over legal deficiencies. Trump's lawsuit said the Rupert Murdoch-owned newspaper tarnished his reputation with an article describing a birthday card to deceased sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as bearing Trump's signature. Trump is seeking at least US$10 billion in damages, according to the amended lawsuit.

items.title: Immigration Department says AI-altered passport photos could lead to legal action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The Immigration Department has urged the public not to use photographs generated or altered using artificial intelligence (AI) technology when applying for Malaysian passports. In a Facebook post, the department said the use of AI-generated images or the submission of false information in passport applications was a serious offence that could lead to action under the Passport Act 1966 (Act 150). “Any false photo or information submitted may result in the application being rejected, and the applicant may face action under existing legal provisions,” the department said.

items.title: Pas Syura Council to decide on ties with Bersatu, says Mohd Amar
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Any decision regarding Pas' relationship with Bersatu will be determined by the party's Majlis Syura Ulama, said Pas vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah. He said the party was still waiting for a full report from the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition leadership before any move could be made. Amar said the Majlis Syura would study the report in detail before making any decision concerning Pas' ties with Bersatu and the future direction of cooperation within PN.

items.title: Missing for three days, Pontian woman, 77, found safe in oil palm plantation
items.summary: Pontian Baru fire and rescue station senior fire officer Mohd Khairul Anwar Ab Ghani said the victim, identified as Nathiah Selamat, was found on the third day of the search and rescue (SAR) operation. The operation commander said the victim was found within a 2.4km radius east of the operation’s incident control post. Mohd Khairul Anwar said the SAR operation involved eight fire and rescue personnel, including the department’s K-9 unit.

items.title: RM762k 'mushroom' drug haul found in plastic bottles, Kelantan house
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The General Operations Force (GOF) seized mushroom-based drugs worth RM762,000 following the arrest of a 32-year-old man in Kok Lanas on May 18. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said personnel conducting surveillance spotted a man by the roadside with his motorcycle. "During inspection, the suspect handed over a plastic bag containing five transparent plastic bottles filled with liquid believed to contain mushroom-based drugs.

items.title: Eversendai bags RM400mil new jobs in Singapore and India
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Eversendai Corp Bhd has secured four new contracts worth a combined RM400 million in Singapore and India, further strengthening its order book and regional presence. In Singapore, the structural steel specialist was awarded a project by Kajima Overseas Asia Singapore Pte Ltd for the Toa Payoh Integrated Development. The company said the building incorporates advanced engineering features, including column-free interiors to maximise usable space.

items.title: Kelantan police say Malaysia-Thailand border remains secure after Pattani shooting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Police have assured the public that security along the Malaysia-Thailand border in Kelantan remains under control despite a recent armed attack targeting Thai police officers in Pattani. According to Utusan Malaysia , Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said security at border entry points had been reinforced through round-the-clock monitoring and patrols to ensure no threats could compromise national sovereignty. He said police remained on high alert over issues such as illegal immigration, cross-border crime and other potential security threats.

items.title: Anwar donates sacrificial cow to Pahang media practitioners
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has donated a sacrificial cow to media practitioners in Pahang for this year’s Aidiladha celebration, in recognition of the role journalists play in connecting the government and the people. Pahang Press chairman Fazli Ahmad said the initiative enabled the distribution of sacrificial meat to journalists and photographers from various print and electronic media organisations, as well as residents of Kampung Anak Air. “Pahang Press would like to express its deepest appreciation to the Prime Minister for donating a cow for the ritual sacrifice, with the meat distributed to media personnel.

items.title: LSH Capital accepts RAC land offer, expands property pipeline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lim Seong Hai Capital Bhd (LSH Capital) has accepted a Letter of Offer from Railway Assets Corp (RAC) to develop two plots of land in Selangor, marking a further expansion of its property development pipeline. In a Bursa Malaysia filing, the construction and property development group said it accepted the offer on May 26, 2026, following its earlier announcement dated May 15, 2026 on the proposed project. LSH Capital's net profit for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q2 FY26) fell to RM18.6 million from RM21.58 million a year earlier, while revenue declined 6.7 per cent to RM99.45 million from RM106.6 million.

items.title: Rehabilitation programme participants fund largest qurban at Sepang centre
items.summary: SEPANG: The Sepang Reintegration Centre held its largest qurban programme to date this Aidiladha, with 49 participants in the rehabilitation programme contributing from their own wages to fund the slaughter of 10 cattle. The figure more than doubled from previous years and grew out of the department's Community Service Integration (CSI) programme, which places participants with selected companies as paid employees. Selangor and Federal Territories deputy director of Prisons Faizal Ahmad said two-thirds of the participants' wages are channelled into savings, with some setting money aside for up to a year to take part in the qurban.

items.title: Search for boy, 10, who fell into Sungai Bernam expanded to three-kilometre radius
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 28 — Search and rescue (SAR) operations for a 10-year-old boy who fell into Sungai Bernam in Kampung Sungai Nilam, Gedangsa, Hulu Selangor entered its second day today, with the search area expanded to a three-kilometre radius. According to Berita Harian , Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the SAR team was still searching for Muhammad Aakif Imran Asrul Nizam as of 11am. “The team is conducting surface searches within a three-kilometre radius from the Fire Control Post (PKB), assisted by other agencies including the Slim River Fire and Rescue Station.

items.title: Thai court dismisses royal insult case against ex-opposition leader
items.summary: BANGKOK: A Thai court on Thursday dismissed a royal defamation case against a former opposition leader over comments he made during the Covid-19 pandemic, in a rare ruling in favour of a prominent government critic. Thailand's strict lese majeste law protects the royal family from criticism and carries jail terms of up to 15 years per offence. At least three former MPs from the opposition Move Forward Party, a successor of Future Forward, have previously been sentenced under the lese majeste law over their political activities.

items.title: Border security tightened following fatal Pattani shooting
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Kelantan police have tightened security along the Malaysia-Thailand border following a fatal shooting in Pattani, southern Thailand, on May 25. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the situation along the border remained under control despite concerns arising from the attack, which claimed the life of a policeman's wife and injured their newborn child. He said police were continuing close cooperation with other security agencies to ensure border areas remained secure and peaceful.

items.title: Body of unidentified man found floating in Sungai Kerayong near Jalan Cheras, believed to be drowned
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The body of a man believed to have drowned was found floating in Sungai Kerayong along Jalan Cheras here on Wednesday. Malay daily Sinar Harian reported Cheras district police chief ACP Mohd Rosdi Daud as saying police received information on the discovery of the unidentified male body at about 5.13pm. Mohd Rosdi said a physical examination by the Kuala Lumpur police forensic team found no signs of foul play on the body.

items.title: Nada targets up to 40,000 drug-related arrests this year
items.summary: SEPANG: The National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) has recorded about 15,000 arrests nationwide so far this year and is targeting between 35,000 and 40,000 arrests by the end of 2026. Its director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said the arrests involved both drug addicts and substance abusers detained under the agency's expanded enforcement powers. "Our target this year is between 35,000 and 40,000 arrests.

items.title: Stats Dept: 81pc employers report disabled workers perform on par with others
items.summary: National news agency Bernama reported that the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) said in its 2025 Employment Survey of Persons with Disabilities that 81 per cent of employers rated PWD workers’ performance on par with other workers, while 71.9 per cent said they contribute to productivity and innovation in the workplace. Nearly 22 per cent of employers currently employ PWD workers. Among current employers, 83.7 per cent employ between one and five PWD workers, with elementary occupations making up 31.4 per cent of roles.

items.title: Dayak community works to keep Gawai heritage alive as traditions fade
items.summary: KUCHING, May 28 — As preparations for Hari Gawai gather pace across Sarawak, concerns are growing over the gradual fading of traditional Dayak customs and rituals amid changing lifestyles and modern influences. Former Sarawak Customs Council research assistant Philip Igai said today’s Gawai celebrations differ greatly from those held between the 1960s and 1980s, when rituals and traditional practices played a much bigger role in the festivities. “In the past, there were more traditional elements such as Gawai Umai and Gawai Tuah, which involved elders and various customary rituals.

items.title: Traffic builds up on major highways nationwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic is building up and moving slowly on several major highways nationwide as of late morning today. On the North-South Expressway Central Link (Elite), northbound traffic was moving slowly from Bandar Gamuda Cove to Putrajaya following an accident at Km25.2, which obstructed the emergency lane. Meanwhile, traffic heading east on the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway was moving slowly near the Gombak toll plaza entry.

items.title: UEC debate must be viewed through national interest lens, says academic
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The debate over recognition of the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) should not be assessed solely on academic merit, but must also take into account the broader national interest and education philosophy. He said, however, that the central policy question was whether the higher education system should formally recognise an examination framework independently designed, administered and governed outside the national education ecosystem. Shaik Abdul Malik, who is also a former chairman of the Malaysia Education Deans' Council, said Malaysia had successfully nationalised its assessment system while maintaining academic standards and international benchmarking.

items.title: Warisan warns against importing ‘divisive’ religious policies into Sabah
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 28 — Warisan has warned against importing what it describes as divisive religious policies from Peninsular Malaysia into Sabah, following controversy over Selangor planning guidelines restricting non-Muslim places of worship from operating in commercial areas and shoplots. Warisan vice-president Datuk Junz Wong said Sabah has had a long-standing culture of moderation, religious tolerance and peaceful coexistence that must not be undermined by policies that risk creating unease among minority communities. “Sabahans deserve to know where Sabah DAP stands on this matter,” he said in a statement, Wednesday.

items.title: PAS veep says party’s ulama council to decide future of Bersatu cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — PAS will leave any decision on its ties with Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia to the party’s ulama council after receiving a full report from Perikatan Nasional (PN) leaders, amid renewed tensions within the Opposition coalition. PAS vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah said the Islamist party had yet to make any decision regarding its relationship with Bersatu, adding that the matter would first be studied by the council once a report was completed. He said the ulama council would scrutinise the report before deciding on PAS’ future cooperation with Bersatu within the PN coalition.

items.title: Forty-five years on, Keda still driving rural transformation in Kedah, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The Kedah Regional Development Authority (KEDA) has continued to play its role as a catalyst agent in accelerating holistic rural transformation throughout its 45 years of establishment since 1981. “Alhamdulillah, KEDA continues to play its role as a catalyst agent in accelerating holistic rural transformation encompassing both worldly and spiritual aspects for the well-being of rural communities. Ahmad Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, expressed hope that KEDA would continue to be a caring, responsive and committed agency in championing the rural development agenda.

items.title: Penang gazettes 50 cultural heritage items in major preservation push
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 28 — Penang has officially gazetted 50 cultural heritage items this year under the Penang State Heritage Enactment 2011 as part of efforts to preserve the state’s rich cultural treasures. State Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said the inaugural gazettement involved 15 heritage sites, seven intangible cultural heritage elements and 28 local heritage food items. He said the move would also pave a stronger path for Penang’s cultural heritage to be elevated to the national level under the National Heritage Act 2005 (Act 645), with the potential to be nominated to Unesco’s Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in the future.

items.title: Rapid KL activates alternative train services, free shuttle buses after derailment near Chan Sow Lin (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Rapid KL today activated alternative train services and free shuttle buses along the Ampang–Sri Petaling Line after a train derailed near Chan Sow Lin station this morning. In a statement, the rail operator said alternative train services had been deployed between Cheras and Bandar Tasik Selatan stations, as well as between Ampang and Pandan Jaya stations. It also said a free shuttle bus service was also operating between Pudu and Pandan Jaya stations.

items.title: Warisan backs proposal for bipartisan MA63 committee
items.summary: LAHAD DATU, May 28 — Tungku Assemblyman Assaffal P. Alian welcomed the proposal to establish a bipartisan committee under the Sabah Legislative Assembly to examine the state’s rights under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63), describing it as a meaningful step for the people of Sabah. Assaffal said the suggestion by Kiulu Assemblyman Joniston Bangkuai reflected a shared commitment to protect Sabah’s constitutional position beyond political lines. 199/1965 [Modification of Laws (Declaration of Federal Present Laws) (Sabah) Order 1965.

items.title: Bersatu denies talks to join Pakatan, says rumours do not make sense
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz has insisted that any cooperation between Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) has never been discussed at any party platform or meeting. On that basis, the Bersatu information chief said claims that the party would join PH through the role allegedly played by the party’s secretary-general, Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali, were completely untrue. Previously, rumours had surfaced that Bersatu could return to PH following tensions in the party’s relationship with PAS.

items.title: Reading builds a more mature society, says Anwar ahead of KL Book Fair launch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the practice of reading can help shape a more mature and broad-minded society in facing an increasingly complex world. In a Facebook post, he said books are not only a source of knowledge but also educate society to be more patient in thinking and wiser in making judgments. “Insya-Allah, tomorrow I will launch the 2026 Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair (PBAKL) as a sign of continued support for the culture of knowledge and reading among the people, particularly the younger generation,” he said.

items.title: Rapid KL confirms train derailment near Chan Sow Lin disrupts Ampang-Sri Petaling Line (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Rapid KL today confirmed that one of its trains derailed while passing a railway switch near Chan Sow Lin station, disrupting services along the Ampang-Sri Petaling Line. Shuttle train services are also operating between Ampang and Maluri via Platform 2 every six minutes, while separate shuttle trains between Maluri and Chan Sow Lin via Platforms 1B and 2B are running at eight-minute intervals. Rapid KL said trains from Putra Heights are turning back at Bandar Tasik Selatan every six minutes, with additional shuttle train services operating between Bandar Tasik Selatan and Cheras, as well as between Cheras and Pudu, at eight-minute intervals.
